frameworks.name: core-knowledge systems
frameworks.one_line: innate domain-specific systems for objects, actions, number, space, and social partners

core_question: What foundational mental systems underlie human cognition, and how do they shape adult thought?
key_assumptions: cognition rests on a small set of innate, domain-specific representational systems
these systems are shared across phylogeny and ontogeny
early-emerging systems persist and constrain adult reasoning
manipulations: comparative synthesis across infants, non-human primates, children, and adults
key_findings: four core systems represent objects, actions, number, and space
a candidate fifth system represents social partners
each system has deep evolutionary and developmental roots
these systems continue to guide adult mental life
interpretations: human cognition is founded on a small set of core knowledge systems
converging evidence across populations can both characterize and help transcend these systems' limits
